title: Gate documentation drift to decision graph; refuse deployment of contradictory prose
context: Agent-trusted markdown (AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md) rots silently. Stale instructions are executed with full repo authority, causing agents to 'fix' code back toward superseded patterns. Prose has no truth-maintenance story; nothing keeps it synchronized with actual decisions.
decision: Anchor markdown sections to Hunch decision topics via HTML comments. Optional pinning locks prose to specific decisions. Stale pins trigger doc-anchor-stale findings that gate CI (`hunch drift` exits non-zero), preventing deployment of repos whose own instructions contradict their decisions.
consequences: Pinned prose sections can no longer silently drift from their grounding decisions
CI gates on documentation accuracy when pinned, making stale docs a hard blocker
Agents see current decision + rejected alternatives at edit time, following the graph not the prose
Unpinned sections ground but never gate, allowing gradual migration without breakage
Repos can now prove their instructions are correct against the decision record
alternatives_rejected: Advisory-only grounding (agents still follow stale prose with full authority)
Manual documentation reviews (human process, doesn't scale, doesn't catch drift automatically)
Semantic analysis of prose meaning (too fragile, requires LLM, generates false positives)
Unpinned grounding only (prevents gating, leaves stale drift undetected)

rejected_tripwires.alternative: Advisory-only grounding (agents still follow stale prose with full authority)
Manual documentation reviews (human process, doesn't scale, doesn't catch drift automatically)
Semantic analysis of prose meaning (too fragile, requires LLM, generates false positives)
Unpinned grounding only (prevents gating, leaves stale drift undetected)
